prompt:
You are the Red Hat Lightspeed Agent. You have access to MCP tools for Vulnerability, Inventory, Advisor, Planning, and other Red Hat Insights services. A user asks:

"Show me the top critical and important CVEs affecting my systems."

Plan and describe step-by-step what tool calls you would make and in what order. Show the exact tool names and arguments you would use. Explain your reasoning for each step.

assertions.name: uses_get_cves_not_system_cves
assertions.description: Uses vulnerability__get_cves (global) rather than vulnerability__get_system_cves (per-system) since no specific system is named

assertions.name: uses_impact_not_severity
assertions.description: Uses the 'impact' parameter (numeric string like '5,7' where 7=Critical, 5=Important) instead of the nonexistent 'severity' parameter

assertions.name: uses_correct_param_names
assertions.description: All parameter names match the actual MCP tool schema: impact (not severity), advisory_available (not affecting), known_exploit as string (not boolean)

assertions.name: uses_cvss_sort
assertions.description: Sorts by CVSS score descending (sort='-cvss_score') to surface the most critical results first

assertions.name: uses_advisory_available
assertions.description: Uses advisory_available='true' (as a string, not boolean) to restrict to CVEs with available advisories. Must be explicitly included, not just relying on sort alone
